meaning: n.狂喜的人adj.狂喜的,心醉神迷的,入迷的
examples: Sally was ecstatic about her new job.
They gave an ecstatic reception to the speech.
His wife gave birth to their first child, and he was ecstatic about it.
"I am ecstatic with the decision, " Ms. Wilford, whose 8-year-old son attends Sandy Hook.
For example, a fancy cafeteria with a celebrity chef might make some employees ecstatic.
